Sports.detail: The New York Yankees exploded for 13 runs in a single inning against the Oakland Athletics, marking their first such offensive outburst in 21 years. This offensive display contributed significantly to their overall victory.
Sports.headline: Yankees Match 21-Year Offensive Milestone With 13-Run Frame Against Athletics
James Milner retires after 24-year Premier League career
Sports.detail: Former England international James Milner has announced his retirement from professional football after a remarkable 24-year career in the Premier League. Milner is known for holding the Premier League appearance record.

The San Antonio Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 111-103 in Game 7 to win the Western Conference title and advance to the NBA Finals. Julian Champagnie, once waived by the 76ers, has emerged as an 'Unsung Hero' for the Finals-bound Spurs.
Sports.headline: Spurs win the West, topple Thunder 111-103 in Game 7 to head to NBA Finals
Alex Palou takes the checkered flag at the Detroit Grand Prix
Sports.detail: Alex Palou, starting from pole position, secured his fourth win of the year at the Detroit Grand Prix. This victory further solidifies his lead in the IndyCar Series championship standings.

Laurie Daley faced immense pressure before the 2026 Origin series, but one journalist argues he now deserves respect after a series-opening victory, despite criticism that Kalyn Ponga's send-off for an ugly tackle significantly aided NSW's comeback. Daley has a 41 per cent win percentage as Blues coach across two stints.
Sports.headline: 'No respect whatsoever': Fiery Daley debate explodes after Origin 'abomination' saved by send off

Science.detail: NASA's futuristic X-59 jet is preparing for its most significant challenge: breaking the sound barrier for the first time, following a series of test flights that approached supersonic speeds. Engineers are making final preparations for this pivotal flight.
Science.headline: NASA's X-59 Is About to Break the Sound Barrier for the First Time
NASA's Roman Telescope Could Reveal 100,000 Hidden Worlds
Science.detail: NASA's Roman Space Telescope has the potential to revolutionize the search for exoplanets, with the possibility of discovering approximately 100,000 exoplanets, surpassing the combined discoveries of all previous missions. The telescope will explore previously unexamined regions of the Milky Way, assisting researchers in their quest.

Science.headline: Why Sweden's Wolverine Conservation Success Story Is Unraveling
Science.detail: A well-known conservation program that was instrumental in saving Sweden's endangered wolverines is currently facing difficulties due to stagnant funding and declining local trust. Researchers caution that this decline emphasizes the need for sustained, long-term commitment to wildlife protection.

Science.headline: Scientists Found the Hidden Switch Fueling Alzheimer's Brain Inflammation
Science.detail: Researchers have discovered a previously unknown mechanism that drives brain inflammation in Alzheimer's disease. This finding could lead to new therapeutic strategies for combating the neurodegenerative condition.

Science.headline: Chimpanzees and Bonobos Have Human-like Friend Circles, Study Finds
Science.detail: By observing grooming behaviors, scientists have found that chimpanzees and bonobos develop intimate inner circles alongside broader networks of weaker social connections. Chimpanzees tend to concentrate on.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Trump might be losing the farm vote
Opinion/Editorial.detail: War in Iran and the trade war are hurting farmers, threatening the Republican heartland coalition as political coalitions are not set in stone and must be won in every cycle. American voters don't have much patience for excuses, especially a flailing economy.

California's governor is ordering state workers to return to in-person work four days a week starting July 1, but unions are fighting the mandate, arguing that limiting telework will undermine California's climate goals. The union contends that returning to the office is a discretionary decision with environmental impacts that must be analyzed and mitigated.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: California state employees are running out of excuses to work from home

Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Ma Ying-jeou Foundation filed criminal complaints against two former employees for breaching trust and embezzlement, leading to speculation about power struggles and Ma's health.  Hsiao Hsu-tsen, a former CEO, hinted at Ma's cognitive decline, while party Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun suggested Ma's family should care for him at home and limit his public appearances.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Ma Ying-jeou: Palace drama hero

Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Tribune-Review presents its editorial cartoons for the week of June 1, 2026, offering visual commentary on current events. These cartoons provide a concise and often humorous perspective on the week's news.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial cartoons for the week of June 1
Secure gun storage will save lives
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece emphasizes the importance of secure gun storage to prevent firearm suicide, accidental shootings, and theft. Secure storage creates a meaningful pause that may allow intense emotions to subside or a loved one to intervene, potentially saving a life.

Technology.headline: NVIDIA's RTX Spark is an AI "superchip" that will power Windows laptops and desktops
Technology.detail: NVIDIA claims the new RTX Spark chip for PCs will offer 1 petaflop of AI computing power, bringing supercomputer-level AI capabilities to consumer laptops and desktops.
The new Surface laptop pairs Nvidia's RTX Spark superchip with Windows on Arm, creating a powerful new AI PC.
Technology.headline: Microsoft's Nvidia-Spark-Powered Surface Laptop Ultra Signals a Raw Power Revolution

Technology.detail: Nvidia's new RTX Spark chip is purpose-built for AI agents and will power a new wave of Windows laptops and desktop PCs coming this fall.
Technology.headline: Nvidia enters Windows AI PC race with new RTX Spark chip: All major announcements at Computex 2026

Technology.detail: NVIDIA announced the new Isaac Gr00t reference design humanoid robot platform that combines humanoid robot, five-fingered hands and NVIDIA Jetson Thor compute.
Technology.headline: NVIDIA's Isaac Gr00t platform gives researchers access to frontier humanoid robotics

Technology.detail: ASUS' new ROG Xbox Ally X20 has mostly the same hardware as last year's handheld, but now comes with a new OLED panel.
Technology.headline: ASUS' ROG Xbox Ally X20 bundle includes a limited-edition OLED Ally X handheld PC and AR gaming glasses

Zimbabwe.detail: Police are mobilizing farmers and miners to participate in an anti-crime initiative. The effort aims to enhance security and reduce criminal activities in farming and mining communities.
Zimbabwe.headline: Police mobilise farmers, miners for anti-crime drive

Zimbabwe.detail: There are concerns about potential procurement corruption as Harare announces plans to purchase 200 buses. The announcement has raised fears about transparency and accountability in the procurement process.
Zimbabwe.headline: Procurement graft fears as Hre announces plans to buy 200 buses

Zimbabwe.detail: Delta Corporation's revenue is expected to increase significantly due to a US$120 million capital expenditure boost. The investment is set to enhance the company's operations and drive revenue growth.
Zimbabwe.headline: Delta revenue poised to soar on US$120m capex blitz

Zimbabwe.detail: The Msasa roundabout will be closed today to facilitate the commencement of work on an interchange. The closure is necessary to allow for the construction of the interchange, which aims to improve traffic flow.
Zimbabwe.headline: Msasa roundabout closes today as work on interchange starts
VP Mohadi urges more investment, legal compliance in Beitbridge
Zimbabwe.detail: Vice President Mohadi is encouraging increased investment and adherence to legal regulations in Beitbridge. He emphasized the importance of investment for the region's growth and the need for compliance with the law.

Crime & Justice.detail: A woman has died after being shot in the eye in the Bronx, according to the NYPD.
Crime & Justice.headline: Woman dies after being shot in the eye in the Bronx, NYPD says
Torture suspects sent texts about sadistic abuse of child, prosecutors say.
Crime & Justice.detail: Prosecutors say that suspects involved in a torture case exchanged text messages detailing the sadistic abuse of a child.
A police sergeant is accused of stalking and repeatedly texting a nurse.
Crime & Justice.headline: 'I'm coming': Police sergeant stalked and repeatedly texted nurse, cops say

Crime & Justice.detail: Police say a husband texted his adult children that he killed his wife with a barbell due to long-simmering hatred.
Crime & Justice.headline: Husband texted adult children he killed wife with barbell over long-simmering hatred: Cops

Crime & Justice.detail: A teenage girl has been arrested and charged for allegedly stabbing three horses during a racing event in Las Vegas.
Crime & Justice.headline: Teen accused of stabbing 3 horses during racing event in Las Vegas

Lifestyle.detail: Forty years after the first cookie, guests keep showing up — sometimes just for the chocolatey, walnut-packed treat.
Lifestyle.headline: How DoubleTree's cookies became one of hospitality's warmest traditions
Miss Manners: Should you applaud at solemn events?
Lifestyle.detail: Advice on etiquette for various situations.
Navigating family dynamics when a relative is grieving.
Lifestyle.headline: Asking Eric: Family drained by a grieving aunt

Lifestyle.detail: Advice on handling interference in a couple's relationship.
Lifestyle.headline: Carolyn Hax: Mother jumps in the middle of couple's decision to sleep apart
Swedish Midsummer: Folk Traditions, Midnight Skiing, Seaside Saunas
Lifestyle.detail: Exploring the traditions and activities associated with Swedish Midsummer.

Entertainment.detail: Pop star Dua Lipa and actor Callum Turner have reportedly tied the knot in a private ceremony in London. The couple has been dating for over two years.
Entertainment.headline: Dua Lipa, Callum Turner say 'I do' in secret London ceremony
Jamie Lee Curtis announces death of actor sister Kelly aged 69: 'My first friend and lifelong confidant'
Entertainment.detail: Jamie Lee Curtis announced the death of her sister, Kelly Curtis, who was also an actress, at the age of 69. Kelly was known for her roles in films like 'The Sentinel'.

Entertainment.headline: Italy bans Kanye West concert over security concerns
Entertainment.detail: Due to concerns regarding public order and safety, Italy has reportedly cancelled a Kanye West concert that was scheduled to take place in the country. The specific reasons for the safety concerns were not detailed.

A collection of rare photos from The Beatles' last ticketed concert 60 years ago in San Francisco has surfaced. The concert occurred before anyone knew it would be their final performance with tickets sold.
Entertainment.headline: Rare photos of Beatles' final ticketed concert
Virat Kohli dances with Anushka Sharma after RCB's IPL 2026 triumph, declares: 'One felt nice, we did it twice'
Entertainment.detail: Following Royal Challengers Bangalore's (RCB) IPL 2026 victory, Virat Kohli was seen dancing with his wife, Anushka Sharma. Kohli expressed his joy, stating, 'One felt nice, we did it twice'.

Finance & Economy.detail: South African food producer Tiger Brands has increased its dividend payout after a 26% jump in operating profit, demonstrating strong financial performance.
Finance & Economy.headline: Tiger Brands hikes dividend as operating profit jumps 26%
Petrol to rise R1.43, while diesel gets some relief
Finance & Economy.detail: South African motorists will face an increase in petrol prices by R1.43, while diesel prices will see some relief, impacting transportation and consumer costs.

Finance & Economy.headline: Big tech is suddenly impacting niche market for US dividends
Finance & Economy.detail: Major technology companies are beginning to exert influence over the U.S. dividend market, affecting investment strategies and returns.

Finance & Economy.headline: India's manufacturing sector growth hits 3-month high in May as domestic market fuels activity: PMI
Finance & Economy.detail: India's manufacturing sector has experienced a surge in growth, reaching a three-month high in May, driven by strong domestic market activity as indicated by the Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI).

Finance & Economy.headline: Copper gains with US tariffs deadline less than a month away
Finance & Economy.detail: Copper prices are increasing as the deadline for US tariffs approaches, reflecting market anticipation and potential economic impacts.

World.detail: Iran and the United States said they had carried out strikes on military targets, and each accused the other of acting aggressively as diplomatic efforts to end three months of war drag on.
World.headline: U.S. bombs Iranian military sites as Kuwait hit by drone, missile fire

World.detail: Colombia's far-right presidential candidate De la Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff, and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeks.
World.headline: Colombia presidential vote headed to run-off between pro-Trump, leftist rivals

World.detail: The French navy, backed by the UK, intercepted a sanctioned tanker from Russia.
World.headline: French navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic
Netanyahu orders Israeli bombing of southern Beirut
World.detail: Israel is targeting southern Beirut as Kuwait condemns Iranian missile and drone attack.

World.headline: Less time wastage, more red cards: New football rules at the World Cup
World.detail: Red cards for covering mouths, corner kicks for time wasting and broadened use of VAR technology will be implemented.

Health.headline: Daily pill can double survival time for world's deadliest cancer, trial shows
Health.detail: A daily pill has shown promising results in a trial, potentially doubling the survival time for patients with pancreatic cancer.

Health.headline: Yoga can reduce anxiety and insomnia for people living with cancer, study finds
Health.detail: A recent study indicates that practicing yoga can help alleviate anxiety and improve sleep quality for individuals undergoing cancer treatment.
An article discusses the aspirations of midwives to create positive childbirth experiences and examines the issues that have arisen in Nottingham.
Health.headline: Midwives want to make childbirth miraculous – so what went so wrong in Nottingham?
Experimental pill promises new hope for deadly pancreatic cancer
Health.detail: An experimental pill is showing promise in providing new hope for treating deadly pancreatic cancer, according to recent medical research news.

Health.headline: Study reveals unexpected health benefits after 5 minutes of prayer
Health.detail: A recent study suggests that even a short period of prayer can lead to surprising and positive health outcomes.

Politics.headline: Ethiopia holds elections with PM Abiy's party expected to dominate
Politics.detail: Voting will not take place in the northern Tigray region and some parts of the Amhara region amid insecurity.

Politics.headline: US says it conducted 'self-defense strikes' on Iranian drone command sites
Politics.detail: The United States carried out further “self-defense” strikes in Iran over the weekend, targeting Iranian radar and command and control sites. U.S. Central Command said the strikes were a response to “aggressive Iranian actions that included the shootdown of a US MQ-1 drone opening over international waters.

Politics.headline: New bill targets domestic abusers and overhauls right to buy in England
Politics.detail: A new bill in the UK aims to address domestic abuse and reform the right to buy scheme in England.
Louisiana Republicans passed a new gerrymander that will eliminate one of the state's two Democratic, majority-Black House districts.
Politics.headline: Louisiana Republicans pass new gerrymander eliminating majority-Black House district
What to know about California's messy governor's race
Politics.detail: California's unusual primary system, combined with scandal and a low-profile field, have made this year's race harder to predict.
